commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Human maintainer review is still needed because the PR is dirty against current main and adds public Plugin SDK API, even though the requester-metadata and changelog defects are mechanically fixable.; Needs attention: The diff still introduces a concrete hook trust-boundary concern by copying caller-controlled requester identity into sanitized-looking plugin task metadata.; Review finding: [P1] Keep requester... (sha=29302ab4123868d33dc107a4ee947c88203a6f65)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a public `sessions_send` plugin hook and SDK types, routes the core `sessions_send` tool through direct or delegated plugin handling, and updates docs, SDK baseline, tests, and `CHANGELOG.md`.

PR surface: Source +382, Tests +332, Docs +4, Generated 0. Total +718 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect: source inspection of the PR head shows caller-provided `task.requester.sessionKey/channel` copied into sanitized hook task metadata before host defaults. The broader change is a proposed new plugin hook, not a current-main bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer review is still needed because the PR is dirty against current main and adds public Plugin SDK API, even though the requester-metadata and changelog defects are mechanically fixable.

target.title: fix: harden sessions_send delegated announce trust
target.body:
## 요약
- clawsweeper P1/P2 지적 대응: `sessions_send` delegated announce 경로에서 caller-supplied `task.runtime.roundOneReply`를 신뢰하지 않도록 제거했습니다.
- delegated announce/ping-pong seed는 plugin-owned `dispatch.roundOneReply` 또는 core가 reply-history wait/read로 검증한 reply만 사용하도록 trust boundary를 고정했습니다.
- `sessions_send` hook 호출을 core reply-history snapshot/read보다 앞에 두어 hook-owned delivery가 로컬 세션 history 가정에 의해 차단되지 않도록 했습니다.

## 테스트
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts` → 2 files / 20 tests passed
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` → OK `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`
- `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm build:plugin-sdk:dts` → passed
- commit hook의 `pnpm check`는 `tsgo:extensions:test` 진행 중 세션 timeout/SIGTERM으로 중단되어, 위 targeted gate와 SDK/type gate를 별도 확보했습니다.

## 리스크
- 기존 PR #68622의 fork branch에 직접 push하지 않고 방통 replacement patch branch로 열었습니다. maintainer가 기존 PR에 cherry-pick하거나 이 PR을 기준으로 이어갈 수 있습니다.
target.author: jinon86

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: goldmar

target.title: fix(googlechat): preserve unresolved service account refs
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve unresolved Google Chat `serviceAccountRef` as an error instead of silently falling back to legacy inline `serviceAccount` credentials.
- Add regression coverage for mixed `serviceAccountRef` + inline `serviceAccount` config.

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Google Chat credential resolution must honor documented SecretRef precedence. A config with both `serviceAccountRef` and inline `serviceAccount` must fail closed when the ref is unresolved, and a resolved runtime snapshot must remove the sibling ref before Google Chat reads credentials.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR worktree on the rebased head `ef2bfcd48f`, using Node/pnpm from the repo environment. I checked env and `~/.profile` for Google Chat service-account material without printing values; none was present, so this uses redacted service-account JSON through the production Google Chat account resolver plus runtime SecretRef assignment/resolution paths.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `pnpm exec tsx` against `extensions/googlechat/src/accounts.ts` and `extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.ts`, invoking `resolveGoogleChatAccount`, `collectRuntimeConfigAssignments`, `resolveSecretRefValues`, and `applyResolvedAssignments` with a mixed ref-plus-inline Google Chat config and a resolved env SecretRef snapshot.

**Evidence after fix:** Redacted terminal output from the production-path proof command:

```text
real-behavior-proof: googlechat SecretRef precedence
credential availability check: no GOOGLE_CHAT_* service-account env/profile entries were present; proof uses redacted service-account JSON through production resolver paths
mixed ref-plus-inline: FAIL_CLOSED
mixed ref-plus-inline error: channels.googlechat.accounts.default.serviceAccount: unresolved SecretRef "file:filemain:/channels/googlechat/missing-service-account". Resolve this command against an active gateway runtime snapshot before reading it.
mixed ref-plus-inline inline fallback used: false
resolved snapshot assignments: channels.googlechat.accounts.prod.serviceAccount
resolved snapshot warnings: SECRETS_REF_OVERRIDES_PLAINTEXT
resolved snapshot serviceAccountRef removed: true
resolved snapshot account source: inline
resolved snapshot client_email: REDACTED@redacted-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
resolved snapshot private_key: REDACTED
resolved snapshot inline legacy used: false
```

**Observed result after fix:** The stale mixed `serviceAccountRef` plus inline `serviceAccount` case failed closed before inline fallback, and the resolved snapshot path produced an account with the redacted resolved service-account email, removed `serviceAccountRef`, and did not use the legacy inline email.

**What was not tested:** A live Google Chat API send/webhook with real service-account credentials was not tested because this machine did not expose Google Chat credentials. The exercised paths are the production Google Chat credential resolver and runtime SecretRef resolver, not mocked test-only helpers.

## Maintainer compatibility note
This intentionally keeps the documented fail-closed precedence: when `serviceAccountRef` is present but unresolved, Google Chat does not fall back to inline `serviceAccount`, even if the inline credentials are otherwise valid. Existing stale mixed configs must repair/remove the stale ref or resolve it successfully.

## Tests
- `pnpm test extensions/googlechat/src/setup.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/googlechat/src/accounts.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/secret-contract.ts extensions/googlechat/src/setup.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json`
- `pnpm check:changed`
